signals.idea:
M2 — Overlay stack. Centralize Base UI portal ownership to harden against the class of bugs that caused the Model Select flash-close (and will re-emerge through other triggers if the mechanism isn't hardened).

## Executor rules

EXECUTOR HARD RULE (authoritative): For any task whose satisfaction requires a browser, runtime app instance, device, or manual human input that the sandboxed executor cannot produce (examples: telemetry overlay capture from a running app, drag/resize smoke checks, keyboard/playback behavior, AgentChat bridge manual test, pending-add URL flow verification, Model Select click behavior, textarea typing verification, any rendering count measured at runtime) — return status=skipped on FIRST encounter with executor_notes evidence naming the blocker (e.g. "no browser-driving path available"). DO NOT return status=blocked. DO NOT retry the task expecting different sandbox capabilities. The review/gate phase already marks these criteria as deferred_human; skipped tasks are first-class evidence that the criterion is deferred to post-milestone human verification. Blocked retries waste iteration budget and do not change outcome.

## Motivation

M1 → M1c eliminated the known render-storm triggers that caused Base UI overlay portals to unmount before their click-outside detection settled. That fixed the immediate Model Select / textarea-typing bugs. But the underlying mechanism — ad-hoc ownership of `body.pointer-events: none`, per-portal focus restoration, and uncoordinated z-index stacking across ~20 portal primitives — still exists. Any future render-storm trigger (new hook, new provider, new lib upgrade) can re-express the same bug class.

M2 hardens the mechanism itself: centralize overlay lifecycle so bugs in this class have one place to fix instead of twenty.

## Goal

Build a single overlay-stack store that wraps every Base UI portal primitive and owns:
1. `document.body.style.pointerEvents` — only the outermost open overlay sets it to 'none'; close order does not matter.
2. Focus restoration — central stack tracks which element had focus at overlay open; restores on close in reverse order.
3. Z-index layering — overlays consult the stack for their layer; no hardcoded z-index across files.

## Scope

### In scope

- `src/shared/state/overlayStack.ts` — Zustand store (using `createEntityStore` factory pattern where appropriate, or a simpler slice if entity-keyed isn't a fit).
- Wrapper components in `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` for each Base UI portal primitive:
  - Dialog, Popover, Select, Menu, Tooltip-with-modal, Drawer, AlertDialog, ContextMenu
- Migrate every callsite to the wrapper. Inventory command:
  `rg 'from .*@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup)' src/ --type ts -l`
  Expect 20+ callsites; actual count must match inventory at milestone start.

### Out of scope

- Replacing Base UI itself.
- Adding new overlay types.
- Touching overlay styling beyond what centralization requires.
- Writing E2E tests (no browser harness in repo).

## Dependencies (technical, not temporal)

- The M1-M1c store substrate must exist in the worktree: specifically `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts` and `src/shared/state/selectionStore.ts`. Verify at milestone start via `ls src/shared/state/`.
- React 18+ (already in repo).
- Base UI portal primitives (already in repo).

## Implementation approach

1. **Inventory** all portal callsites via the grep above. Document each with its current z-index, focus-restoration behavior (if any), and pointer-events ownership.
2. **Build `overlayStack.ts`** — store with actions: `pushOverlay({ id, type, restoreFocusTo })`, `popOverlay(id)`, `getTopOverlay()`. Selector: `useIsOverlayTopmost(id)`.
3. **Build wrapper components** — one per primitive. Each wrapper:
   - Calls `pushOverlay` on mount; `popOverlay` on unmount.
   - Reads `useIsOverlayTopmost(id)` to decide whether to own body pointer-events.
   - Uses stack-layered z-index via a CSS variable pegged to stack depth.
4. **Migrate callsites** — one primitive type at a time. Keep prop API identical so consumer code doesn't change. Delete unused per-callsite z-index overrides and focus-management code as wrappers land.
5. **Regression harness** — integration tests (RTL/Vitest) that open/close overlay sequences and assert `document.body.style.pointerEvents === ''` after all overlays close.

## MUST (all automated)

- `npx tsc --noEmit` clean on the branch.
- `npm test` passes at M0/M1c baseline count or higher.
- No portal primitive imports remain outside `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` except in the wrappers themselves. Verify: `rg 'from .*@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup)' src/ --type ts -l | grep -v 'ui/overlay/'` returns empty.
- Integration tests covering open-A → open-B → close-A → close-B sequence for at least Dialog+Popover and Menu+Dialog pairings. Asserts `document.body.style.pointerEvents === ''` after full close.
- `overlayStack.ts` exports a public API documented with JSDoc on each action.
- No `z-index: [number]` literals in any migrated overlay wrapper file; all layering flows through the stack.

## DEFERRED_HUMAN (skipped by executor, verified manually post-milestone)

- Nested overlay sequences: LoRA modal → Model Select inside it; close LoRA → Model Select should close cleanly with no focus stolen, no body pointer-events stuck.
- Drag-while-overlay-open: open any overlay, drag a clip in timeline — overlay stays open, drag works, no click-outside false-positive dismissing overlay.
- Tab key traversal inside overlay stays trapped; Escape closes topmost; focus returns to opener.

## Non-goals / explicit choices

- No migration of non-Base-UI overlays (our own bespoke modals) unless they already use the same portal plumbing.
- No accessibility audit beyond focus-restoration correctness (different milestone if needed).
- No animation changes — this milestone is plumbing, not polish.

## Risk notes

- 20+ callsite migration is the largest-surface milestone in the chain. If a wrapper diverges from Base UI's prop API, all consumers of that primitive break. Keep wrappers thin.
- `document.body.style.pointerEvents` is a shared global; any non-migrated Base UI primitive opened during this milestone could conflict with the stack. Recommend landing all primitives in one PR.

unresolved_flags.concern: Overlay layering: Recurring debt: call-site z-index overrides remain outside the migration contract, so central wrapper ownership would still be bypassed by `TooltipContent`, `SelectContent`, and `DialogContent` consumers that currently pass `z-[...]`, `overlayClassName`, or `zIndexBase`.

Lightbox overlay ownership: the plan does not explicitly retire the existing DOM-z-index precedence helpers, leaving `overlayStack` and `uiLayers` as competing sources of truth for which overlay is topmost.

Overlay scope: the repository still has a live portal-bearing `preview-card` wrapper in `hover-card.tsx`, so the plan's current primitive list may leave one of the same portal ownership mechanisms outside the hardening pass.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the milestone brief against the actual overlay surface in `src/shared/components/ui/*` and the plan's proposed migration list. The repo still has a portal-bearing `hover-card.tsx` built on `@base-ui/react/preview-card` with a hardcoded `z-[100001]`, and that component is used in live lightbox-adjacent flows such as `AdjacentSegmentNavigation.tsx`, `ConstituentImageNavigation.tsx`, and `VariantCard.tsx`; the plan raises this in metadata as an open question instead of resolving whether it is intentionally out of scope, so the centralization brief is only partially addressed.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the user requirement that z-index ownership stop being hardcoded across files, not just inside wrappers. Current call sites still override overlay layering directly through props and classes, for example `LoraSelectorModal.tsx` uses `overlayClassName="!z-[100012]"` and `zIndexBase={UI_Z_LAYERS.LIGHTBOX_MODAL + 2}`, `sharedSections.tsx` passes `className="z-[100001]"` to `SelectContent`, and many `TooltipContent` callers in the lightbox and variant selector pass `z-[100001]` or `z-[100010]`; the plan does not make those call-site overrides a first-class cleanup target, so it only partially satisfies the approved requirement that layering flow through the central stack.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the current nested-modal path in `LoraSelectorModal.tsx`. That caller depends on `DialogContent`'s `zIndexBase` and `overlayClassName` escape hatches to sit above the lightbox, and it closes itself only when `getTopDialogElement() === contentRef.current`; the plan says those z-index escape hatches should probably be removed and wrapper-local layering should come from the new stack, but it never explains how this specific caller will keep its above-lightbox ordering or how the top-dialog check will continue to work once the rendered topmost element may no longer be the same content node reference.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the existing lightbox interaction logic in `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts`, `useLightboxNavigation.ts`, and `uiLayers.ts`. Those paths currently gate outside-click and Escape behavior by scanning DOM elements marked with `data-dialog-backdrop`, `data-dialog-content`, and `data-lightbox-popup` and comparing computed z-index values; if M2 introduces `overlayStack` as the new source of truth but leaves these DOM-scanning guards in place, the app will have two competing overlay-order mechanisms, which is a correctness risk rather than just an implementation detail.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Searched for other portal-based overlay primitives and found that the broader repo pattern extends beyond the six Base UI families named in the implementation plan. `hover-card.tsx` wraps `@base-ui/react/preview-card`, portals through its own `Positioner`, and hardcodes a z-index, so the underlying 'centralize portal ownership' problem is broader than the plan's current wrapper list even though the brief text only explicitly named dialog/popover/select/menu/tooltip/alert-dialog/context-menu/popup.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Searched for overlay consumers rather than just primitive definitions and found heavy layering customizations in downstream callers, especially under `src/domains/media-lightbox/` and `src/shared/components/VariantSelector/`. The plan's assumption that most consumers can stay untouched behind compatibility re-exports is optimistic because many of the current behavior differences live in those callers through `container`, `className`, and z-index overrides, so the broader issue is not confined to moving raw Base UI imports into one folder.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked the supporting infrastructure around overlay ordering and found non-wrapper code that the plan does not explicitly migrate: `src/shared/lib/uiLayers.ts` and `uiLayers.test.ts`, `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts`, `useLightboxNavigation.ts`, and `LoraSelectorModal.tsx` all encode dialog precedence in terms of DOM markers and fixed z-layers. Without naming those files as part of the migration, the repo will still contain a second overlay-order system after `overlayStack` lands.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked the test surface and confirmed the existing automated coverage is currently limited to `dialog.test.tsx`, `select.test.tsx`, `tooltipPrimitives.test.tsx`, and `uiLayers.test.ts`, with no existing menu integration file. The plan does identify the need for new stack and menu/dialog tests, so the testing direction is reasonable, but the supporting `uiLayers` tests also need either replacement or deliberate preservation because they currently codify the old DOM-z-index strategy.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the callers of the current overlay wrappers and found that several of them pass nontrivial arguments that the migration has to preserve exactly: `SelectContent` is passed `container={container}` in LoRA and lightbox edit-mode flows so the popup portals into the correct subtree, `PopoverContent` is also passed `container` in selectors and AI input controls, and `DialogContent` is passed custom `style`, `overlayClassName`, and `zIndexBase` in modal flows. The plan says 'keep prop API identical' but only explicitly calls out `command.tsx` and `LightboxShell`, so it does not yet show how these concrete caller contracts will be handled once layering is centralized.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 20.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 12.

warnings: Scope creep detected: the plan appears to be expanding beyond the original idea or recorded user notes.

items.criterion: `src/shared/state/overlayStack.ts` exists, exports documented JSDoc actions/selectors for push/pop/topmost/layer access, and centralizes body pointer-events plus focus restoration logic.
No raw `@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup)` imports remain anywhere under `src/` outside `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/`.
Representative integration tests cover `open A -> open B -> close A -> close B` for both Dialog+Popover and Menu+Dialog, and assert `document.body.style.pointerEvents === ''` after full close.
The new overlay stack logic and wrapper migration pass `npx tsc --noEmit`.
`npm test` passes after the overlay migration.
No migrated overlay wrapper file in `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` contains a literal numeric `z-index` value; layering is derived from the central stack.
Existing public wrapper APIs remain source-compatible for current consumers, so most downstream files do not need behavioral rewrites beyond direct raw Base UI import sites.
The overlay stack implementation stays small and mechanical rather than recreating entity-store persistence abstractions that overlays do not use.

Deferred manual checks pass in a real app session: nested LoRA modal + Model Select closes cleanly, drag interactions do not spuriously dismiss overlays, and focus trap/Escape/restore behavior feels correct.
